questions.question: MLflow tracking records:
options: Parameters, metrics, artifacts, and code version for each training run
questions.explanation: Full experiment provenance: what you tried, how it performed, what files were produced.

questions.question: An MLflow Experiment organises:
options: A collection of related runs (e.g. all attempts at tuning a fraud detector)
questions.explanation: Group related runs under one experiment for easy comparison.

questions.question: mlflow.log_metric() records:
options: Numeric values that can change over time (loss, accuracy, AUC) per step
questions.explanation: Metrics are numeric and time-series. Params are string key-value pairs logged once.

questions.question: MLflow Model Registry provides:
options: Versioning, staging, and lifecycle management for production models
questions.explanation: Registry tracks which model version is in production, allows staged rollouts.

questions.question: mlflow.autolog() automatically:
options: Logs framework-specific params/metrics/models without any manual code
questions.explanation: mlflow.sklearn.autolog() captures all sklearn run parameters and metrics automatically.

questions.question: The MLflow UI is accessed via:
options: mlflow ui (local) or hosted platforms like Databricks, SageMaker
questions.explanation: Browser-based dashboard showing experiment trees, metric plots, and artifact browsers.

questions.question: An MLflow artifact is:
options: Any file saved during a run: model files, plots, data samples, reports
questions.explanation: log_artifact('confusion_matrix.png') saves files alongside metrics for later reference.

questions.question: MLflow Projects package:
options: Code + environment + entry points into a reproducible, shareable format
questions.explanation: MLproject file + conda.yaml = anyone can reproduce your experiment with mlflow run .

questions.question: Logging git commit hash in MLflow helps with:
options: Linking any run to the exact code that produced it -- for perfect reproducibility

questions.question: Comparing runs in MLflow helps with:
options: Identifying which hyperparameters, data versions, or architectures produced the best results
questions.explanation: Select multiple runs in UI, compare metric plots and param tables side by side.
